title: Bump github.com/containers/storage from 1.32.3 to 1.32.4
body:
Bumps [github.com/containers/storage](https://github.com/containers/storage) from 1.32.3 to 1.32.4.
Release notes

Sourced from github.com/containers/storage's releases.

v1.32.4

Vendor in opencontainers/runc v1.0.0
overlay: fix check for rootless native diff
Commits
  • feb65b4 Bump to v1.32.4
  • d5e860c Merge pull request #945 from rhatdan/runc
  • af17ac5 Merge pull request #948 from giuseppe/fix-check-for-rootless-native-diff
  • b3531c3 Vendor in opencontainers/runc v1.0.0
  • 1e81ec1 overlay: fix check for rootless native diff
  • b83d218 Merge pull request #944 from rhatdan/version
  • 0eb97d2 Move to v1.32.4-dev
  • See full diff in compare view

[![Dependabot compatibility score](https://dependabot-badges.githubapp.com/badges/compatibility_score?dependency-name=github.com/containers/storage&package-manager=go_modules&previous-version=1.32.3&new-version=1.32.4)](https://docs.github.com/en/github/managing-security-vulnerabilities/about-dependabot-security-updates#about-compatibility-scores) Dependabot will resolve any conflicts with this PR as long as you don't alter it yourself. You can also trigger a rebase manually by commenting `@dependabot rebase`. [//]: # (dependabot-automerge-start) [//]: # (dependabot-automerge-end) ---
Dependabot commands and options
You can trigger Dependabot actions by commenting on this PR: - `@dependabot rebase` will rebase this PR - `@dependabot recreate` will recreate this PR, overwriting any edits that have been made to it - `@dependabot merge` will merge this PR after your CI passes on it - `@dependabot squash and merge` will squash and merge this PR after your CI passes on it - `@dependabot cancel merge` will cancel a previously requested merge and block automerging - `@dependabot reopen` will reopen this PR if it is closed - `@dependabot close` will close this PR and stop Dependabot recreating it. You can achieve the same result by closing it manually - `@dependabot ignore this major version` will close this PR and stop Dependabot creating any more for this major version (unless you reopen the PR or upgrade to it yourself) - `@dependabot ignore this minor version` will close this PR and stop Dependabot creating any more for this minor version (unless you reopen the PR or upgrade to it yourself) - `@dependabot ignore this dependency` will close this PR and stop Dependabot creating any more for this dependency (unless you reopen the PR or upgrade to it yourself)

comments.body: Going to handle this under the runc PR.

OK, I won't notify you again about this release, but will get in touch when a new version is available. If you'd rather skip all updates until the next major or minor version, let me know by commenting `@dependabot ignore this major version` or `@dependabot ignore this minor version`. You can also ignore all major, minor, or patch releases for a dependency by adding an [`ignore` condition](https://docs.github.com/en/code-security/supply-chain-security/configuration-options-for-dependency-updates#ignore) with the desired `update_types` to your config file.

If you change your mind, just re-open this PR and I'll resolve any conflicts on it.
@giuseppe @vrothberg @baude @nalind Any idea what is going wrong here?

Just a guess, but https://github.com/containers/storage/pull/945/files#diff-4721341834f5eb066bda6666f28042a805906eb2a5444a9190ca9db9c96c00daR53 can now set `options.InUserNS` to `false` when the value passed in by its parent process is `true`.
`userns.RunningInUserNS()` looks in `/proc` to determine its answer, and a chrooted child process isn't likely to have a useful `/proc` after it `chroot()`s.

A newer version of github.com/containers/storage exists, but since this PR has been edited by someone other than Dependabot I haven't updated it. You'll get a PR for the updated version as normal once this PR is merged.
@edsantiago Could you look at the `bud` failures.  We need an updated patch.
Try saving this as your new [test/buildah-bud/buildah-tests.diff](https://github.com/containers/podman/files/6728056/buildah-tests.diff.txt)

Ohhhh.... it's that other problem I mentioned, the one with the new "copy" helper. As I mentioned in https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues/3297 this is going to be a gigantic nightmare to fix. I'll try to look into it some time this week. Maybe I'll take another week of PTO and hope that someone else takes care of it.
@edsantiago We did not want you to feel like you weren't missed, so we had to have a huge problem when you came back.

Failure looks like a real one. Is this test (`with-rusage-logfile`) a new one? Does podman have this `--rusage-logfile` flag?
```
[+0666s] not ok 190 bud with-rusage-logfile
         # (from function `die' in file tests/helpers.bash, line 244,
         #  in test file tests/bud.bats, line 2451)
         #   `if [ ! -e "foo.log" ]; then die "foo.log not present!"; fi' failed
         # /var/tmp/go/src/github.com/containers/podman/test-buildah-v1.21.1-0.20210628174543-eadb10a12336/tests /var/tmp/go/src/github.com/containers/podman/test-buildah-v1.21.1-0.20210628174543-eadb10a12336
         # # [checking for: docker.io/library/alpine]
         # # [restoring from cache: /var/tmp/buildah-image-cache.4065 / docker.io/library/alpine]
         # Getting image source signatures
         # Copying blob sha256:9d16cba9fb961d1aafec9542f2bf7cb64acfc55245f9e4eb5abecd4cdc38d749
         # Copying config sha256:961769676411f082461f9ef46626dd7a2d1e2b2a38e6a44364bcbecf51e66dd4
         # Writing manifest to image destination
         # Storing signatures
         # $ podman build --force-rm=false --log-rusage --rusage-logfile foo.log --layers --pull=false --format docker --signature-policy /var/tmp/go/src/github.com/containers/podman/test-buildah-v1.21.1-0.20210628174543-eadb10a12336/tests/policy.json /var/tmp/go/src/github.com/containers/podman/test-buildah-v1.21.1-0.20210628174543-eadb10a12336/tests/bud/shell
         # STEP 1/2: FROM alpine
         # 0s(system) 0s(user) 51ms(elapsed) 0B input 0B output
         # STEP 2/2: SHELL [ "/bin/sh", "-c" ]
         # COMMIT
         # --> 3ffe4b14574
         # 0s(system) 0s(user) 39ms(elapsed) 0B input 0B output
         # 3ffe4b14574a05e038b3ef6a343487b9cb765671965b85dc7dbcb1eb9478ffc5
         # #/vvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvv
         # #| FAIL: foo.log not present!
         # #\^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
         # /var/tmp/go/src/github.com/containers/podman/test-buildah-v1.21.1-0.20210628174543-eadb10a12336
```
Recommendation:
* If podman does have this flag: investigate why this test isn't working
* If podman DOES NOT have this flag (i.e. it's something new): file a podman issue, then `skip` the test in `test/buildah-bud/apply-podman-deltas` with a big 'FIXME: #new-issue-number`
@edsantiago Sad trombone.  Still sees new buildah stuff?

> Still sees new buildah stuff?

@rhatdan I'm having trouble parsing that. Could you rephrase? And, can you confirm that you saw my "Failure looks like a real one" comment above?

Yay, tests green! (And I'm amused to note that @rhatdan completely ignored my suggestions, opting instead to just go ahead and implement the missing option outright without bothering with a separate PR.)

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *dependabot[bot]*, *rhatdan* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [rhatdan] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
@edsantiago I am just happy to push this beast across the finish line.
Great work, folks!  I was just looking up this PR when Dan merged.  This will unblock common merging :)
